periods.title: Boca Chica Field during the wartime expansion era
periods.summary: A public-domain U.S. Navy aerial photograph shows Naval Air Station Key West at Boca Chica during the 1940s. The image is a perspective photograph rather than an orthophoto, so its initial placement is approximate and user-adjustable.
highlights: The source is a U.S. Navy aerial published in Naval Aviation News in September 1947 and hosted by Wikimedia Commons.
It documents the wartime aviation landscape east of Key West and complements the island-focused shoreline story.
Use the nudge, scale, and rotation controls for visual comparison only; perspective distortion prevents survey-grade alignment.

overlay.title: 1940s Boca Chica Field aerial — experimental fit
overlay.attribution: U.S. Navy / Wikimedia Commons, 1940s; public domain

periods.title: Key West shoreline near Bertha Street in the 1950s
periods.summary: A Florida Keys History Center aerial photograph shows the Bertha Street and Atlantic Boulevard shoreline during the 1950s. It is hosted by Wikimedia Commons and replaces the Florida Memory files that could not be fetched reliably by Netlify.
highlights: The source is cataloged as MM00009236 and dated broadly to the 1950s rather than to a specific year.
The photograph provides direct mid-century evidence for the southeastern Key West shoreline and urban edge.
The image remains adjustable because perspective and an uncertain camera position prevent precise georeferencing.

overlay.title: 1950s Bertha Street shoreline aerial — experimental fit
overlay.attribution: Florida Keys Public Libraries / Wikimedia Commons, c.1950s, CC BY 2.0
